title: They Saw Again The Crocus Bloom
content:
They saw again the crocus bloom,
    And, leaning from that lofty room,
    Sir Launcelot with face of gloom
    Look down to Camelot.
    Up flew their veils and floated wide,
    But Livy pinned them to her side,
    'The curse has come upon us!' cried
    The ladies of Shalott.
meaning:
Louisa May Alcott's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "They Saw Again The Crocus Bloom"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
